general.cookieText: We use cookies to improve your experience, analyze site usage, and support our services. By continuing to use this site, you agree to our use of cookies.

filters.title: Filters
filters.startDateError: Start date must be before end date!
filters.minMustBeLess: Min must be less than Max

aboutGeoJsonAndSuperCluster.title: Map Architecture (GeoJSON + Supercluster)
aboutGeoJsonAndSuperCluster.description: This implementation uses a client-driven spatial rendering strategy based on GeoJSON payloads combined with Supercluster running inside a Web Worker.
aboutGeoJsonAndSuperCluster.clientSideRenderingText: All clustering logic runs in the browser. The backend only returns raw spatial data filtered by bounding box.

whatHappensOnInteraction.title: What Happens On Interaction:
whatHappensOnInteraction.2: Frontend sends bounding box + zoom to API.
whatHappensOnInteraction.3: Backend queries PostGIS using spatial index.

advantages.title: Advantages
advantages.1: High FPS rendering (GPU accelerated via Mapbox)
advantages.5: Fast local rebuilds of cluster index

tradeOffs.title: Trade-offs:
tradeOffs.1: Large GeoJSON payloads (can reach several MB)
tradeOffs.3: Client CPU usage during cluster rebuild
tradeOffs.4: Hard to scale beyond ~100k visible points

aboutGeoJsonAndSuperCluster.productionNoteText: For massive datasets, a Vector Tile (MVT) server-side architecture is more scalable, moving clustering and geometry clipping to PostGIS.

aboutVectorTiles.title: Map Architecture (PostGIS → MVT → Mapbox)
aboutVectorTiles.description: This implementation uses server-generated Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT), where geometry clipping and data preparation are performed inside PostgreSQL using PostGIS.
aboutVectorTiles.serverSideSpatialText: PostGIS generates vector tiles using ST_AsMVT and ST_AsMVTGeom. The frontend renders only binary tile data.

whatHappensOnInteraction.title: What Happens On Interaction:
whatHappensOnInteraction.3: Backend generates vector tile in PostGIS.
whatHappensOnInteraction.5: Mapbox renders geometry directly from GPU buffers.

whenThisIsIdeal.title: When This Architecture Is Ideal:
whenThisIsIdeal.1: Hundreds of thousands to millions of points

performance.title: Performance Characteristics:
performance.1: Rendering depends on visible tiles only
performance.3: Backend performance tied to spatial index quality

aboutVectorTiles.productionStandardText: This is the architecture used by large-scale geo platforms such as map providers, logistics systems, and spatial analytics tools.

about.description: Is an analytical system designed to process and visualize a massive dataset of earthquake events (~800,000 records).

projectFocusOn.title: The project serves as a demonstration of high-performance engineering, specifically focusing on:
projectFocusOn.1_body: Handling and processing large-scale datasets efficiently.
projectFocusOn.4_body: Ensuring fast response times for deep-data retrieval.
projectFocusOn.5_body: Rendering thousands of data points without UI lag.
projectFocusOn.6_body: Implementation of caching and background tasks.

about.notACrudText: This project is not a standard CRUD application. Its primary goal is to showcase architectural and engineering solutions applied to a complex, real-world dataset.

generalArchitecture.title: General Architecture
generalArchitecture.description: The system is built with a modern, scalable stack designed to handle high-frequency data operations and complex spatial analysis.
generalArchitecture.3_body: PostgreSQL with PostGIS extension (for spatial data)

interactionWorkflow.title: Interaction Workflow
interactionWorkflow.1_body: Communicates with NestJS API via REST.
interactionWorkflow.2_body: Manages business logic and queries PostgreSQL.
interactionWorkflow.3_body: Acts as a high-speed layer for caching and managing the job queue.
interactionWorkflow.4_body: Processes heavy data tasks via BullMQ to keep the main API responsive.

aboutAuthor.title: About the Author
aboutAuthor.description_1: This project was designed and developed by
aboutAuthor.description_2: as a demonstration of full-stack engineering expertise, focusing on high-performance data systems, spatial analytics, and scalable architecture. The goal of this platform is not just feature implementation, but architectural clarity, performance optimization, and production-grade engineering practices.

about.engineeringFocusText: The emphasis of this project is on system design, performance scaling, data-heavy architecture, and production-level code quality — not just UI development.

metadata.dashboardDescription: Earthquake dashboard with aggregated metrics, advanced filtering, and high-performance data table for large-scale seismic records.
metadata.analyticsDescription: Analytical dashboard displaying average earthquake metrics and time-series trends for large-scale seismic datasets.
metadata.mapDescription: Global earthquake map visualizing up to 150,000+ seismic events with zoom-based data rendering and spatial optimization.
metadata.tilesMapDescription: Global earthquake map visualizing up to 150,000+ seismic events with zoom-based data rendering and spatial optimization.
metadata.aboutDescription: High-performance seismic intelligence platform processing 800K+ earthquake records with spatial queries, caching, and scalable full-stack architecture.
